description: LinkedIn-native lead capture → enrich → score → draft → approve → send pipeline

extractedRequirements: OAuth connection to LinkedIn ad accounts and event forms
Webhook-first real-time lead ingestion with HMAC verification
Contact-first matching with email > LinkedIn URL > domain heuristics
Custom form fields map to contact custom fields
Follow-up email draft referencing campaign/form context
Slack approval with send channel choice (Gmail/Outlook or Instantly)
Autonomy progression: 5 clean approvals → auto-send prompt
extractedConstraints: Reuse existing OAuth initiate router pattern
Reuse emailDraftApproval adapter for Slack HITL
Reuse unified autonomy resolver for progression
webhook-linkedin must be verify_jwt=false with HMAC-SHA256 verification
LinkedIn API requires explicit field projection
Webhook notifications may duplicate — dedup by notification ID
answeredQuestions: CRM model: contact-first, no separate leads table
Multi-org: supported, routing by subscription ID
Send channel: user choice (Gmail/Outlook or Instantly)
Webhook: dedicated public function with HMAC

team.tierReason: 16 stories, 2 schema changes, external API (LinkedIn OAuth + webhooks), security surface (HMAC, tokens, PII), reuses battle-tested patterns

stories.title: Database migration: LinkedIn tables + contact source columns
acceptance: linkedin_lead_sources table created with org_id, form_id, source_type, campaign_name, is_active
linkedin_sync_runs table created with org_id, run_type, lead counts, timestamps
contacts table gains: linkedin_lead_source_id, linkedin_lead_payload (JSONB), linkedin_lead_received_at
RLS policies on all new tables (org-scoped)
Migration is idempotent (DROP POLICY IF EXISTS before CREATE POLICY)

stories.title: OAuth initiate provider: linkedin.ts
acceptance: New file oauth-initiate/providers/linkedin.ts following hubspot.ts pattern
Validates user JWT + org admin/owner role
Creates CSRF state in linkedin_oauth_states (10min TTL) or reuses hubspot_oauth_states pattern
Returns authorization_url with scopes: r_marketing_leadgen_automation, r_events, r_organization_admin
Registered in oauth-initiate/index.ts router

stories.title: LinkedIn OAuth callback handler
acceptance: New edge function linkedin-oauth-callback/index.ts
Validates CSRF state from linkedin_oauth_states
Exchanges authorization code for access + refresh tokens
Stores credentials in integration_credentials (provider='linkedin')
Creates/updates linkedin_org_integrations row with connection metadata
Redirects to frontend with ?linkedin_status=connected or ?linkedin_error=...
config.toml entry: verify_jwt = false

stories.title: Webhook endpoint: webhook-linkedin with HMAC verification
acceptance: New edge function webhook-linkedin/index.ts with verify_jwt=false
HMAC-SHA256 signature verification using X-LI-Signature header + app clientSecret
Idempotency: dedup by LinkedIn notification ID (stored in sync_runs or separate dedup table)
Explicit field projection for formResponse, submittedAt, ownerInfo, associatedEntityInfo
Handles both SPONSORED and EVENTS leadType
Stores raw payload, routes org by webhook subscription ID
Enqueues async processing (does not block on enrichment/scoring/drafting)
Returns 200 to LinkedIn quickly

stories.title: Frontend: useLinkedInIntegration hook + Integrations page UI
acceptance: useLinkedInIntegration hook with connectLinkedIn(), disconnectLinkedIn(), connectionStatus
LinkedIn card on Integrations page with Connect/Disconnect button
Handles ?linkedin_status=connected URL param with success toast
Shows connection health: connected, disconnected, error, token expiry
Uses Lucide LinkedIn icon (not emoji)

stories.title: Lead source configuration UI (form/event selection)
acceptance: Settings panel showing available LinkedIn lead gen forms and event forms
Toggle to enable/disable sync per form
Fetches form list from LinkedIn API via edge function
Writes selected forms to linkedin_lead_sources table
Registers webhook subscription for each enabled form

stories.title: Contact/company matching + dedup on lead ingestion
acceptance: Match priority: email exact → LinkedIn URL → domain + company name
Creates new contact if no match, links to campaign/form metadata
Creates new company if domain not found (skips personal domains)
Ambiguous matches (multiple partial hits) flagged for review, not auto-merged
Source metadata written to contact: linkedin_lead_source_id, linkedin_lead_payload, linkedin_lead_received_at
Custom form question/answer pairs stored as contact custom fields

stories.title: LinkedIn enrichment auto-trigger on lead ingestion
acceptance: After contact match/create, fire-and-forget call to linkedinEnrichmentService
Enrichment runs async (5-15s) — does not block downstream scoring/drafting
If LinkedIn URL available from form data, skip URL discovery step
Enrichment results stored in contacts.social_media_urls (existing pattern)
Failure does not block pipeline — scoring/drafting proceed with available data

stories.title: ICP scoring + owner routing for LinkedIn leads
acceptance: ICP score 0-100 using: company size, role seniority, industry, domain, form context
Incorporates enrichment data if available (non-blocking wait with timeout)
Routing rules: territory → campaign → fallback owner
High-fit leads (>70) optionally create a deal
Low-fit leads create contact + tasks only
Score stored on command centre item context for prioritisation

stories.title: First follow-up email draft generation
acceptance: Generates personalized follow-up referencing campaign/form/event context
Uses org voice settings + owner writing style (user_tone_settings)
CTA matches lead intent: demo request, content follow-up, event follow-up
Draft stored in hitl_pending_approvals with resource_type='linkedin_lead_email'
Em-dashes stripped (existing pattern)
Tier-1 Sonnet for first draft, can fall back to Gemini

stories.title: Task work package creation
acceptance: Creates follow-up task linked to contact/company/deal
Creates research/prep task if high-value company
CRM hygiene task only when material update needed
Tasks due within configurable SLA (default: follow-up 1h, research 24h)
Uses create-task-unified pattern

stories.title: Slack approval flow for LinkedIn lead responses
acceptance: Slack DM with: lead summary, ICP score badge, campaign context, email preview
Buttons: Approve & Send, Edit Draft, Reassign, Dismiss
Send channel selector: Gmail/Outlook or Instantly
Notification dedup: 4hr cooldown per lead (existing dedupe.ts pattern)
Approval logged in hitl_pending_approvals
If Slack not connected, item queued in command centre

stories.title: Email send execution (Gmail/Outlook + Instantly)
acceptance: On approve: sends via email-send-as-rep (Gmail/Outlook) or Instantly based on user choice
Email includes threading headers if replying to existing thread
Instantly push includes custom variables from form data
Send logged in email_send_log with linkedin_lead source
Failure notification sent back to Slack

stories.title: Autonomy progression for LinkedIn lead responses
acceptance: Records autopilot_signals on every approve/reject/edit action
Tracks consecutive clean approvals per user for 'linkedin_lead_email' action type
After 5 consecutive no-edit approvals, sends autonomy promotion Slack DM
Promotion uses existing autonomy_promote_approve/dismiss pattern
Auto-send applies configurable delay (default 5min) with cancel window
Any edit or rejection resets counter
Autonomy level visible in settings, revocable

stories.title: LinkedIn lead ingest orchestrator (main pipeline function)
acceptance: Orchestrates: normalize → match → enrich → score → route → draft → tasks → notify
Each step is fault-tolerant: failure in enrichment/scoring/drafting does not block contact creation
Records linkedin_sync_runs with counts and duration
Logs errors with toast-friendly messages
Respects autonomy level: auto-send if permitted, otherwise HITL

stories.title: Reconciliation polling job for missed webhooks
acceptance: Edge function linkedin-lead-reconcile triggered by cron (every 15min)
Polls LinkedIn leadFormResponses API with field projection
Compares against linkedin_sync_runs to find missed leads
Feeds missed leads into same ingest pipeline
Records reconciliation run in linkedin_sync_runs with run_type='poll_reconciliation'
